title: UDLM Resource Type Specification
description: Meta-schema that every Resource Type Specification in the UDLM Resource Type Registry MUST validate against. JSON Schema 2020-12 is the normative wire format for entity-type definitions (contracts/schema-sharing.md). A Resource Type Specification is the versioned, formal definition of a Resource Type: field schema, constraints, lifecycle rules, portability classification, typed realized outputs, and allowed relationship types (entities/resource-type-hierarchy.md). This meta-schema is itself part of the UDLM spec and is versioned on the SPEC axis.

$id.description: Canonical, project-controlled identifier on udlm.dev (HTTPS-enforced via the .dev HSTS preload). Encodes BOTH version axes and mirrors `conformsTo`: https://udlm.dev/registry/udlm/<spec>/<ResourceType>/<version>. Relationships reference a target by its `$id` (never bare name). A new MAJOR mints a new `$id`.

conformsTo.description: SPEC-axis binding: the UDLM spec major.minor this type is written against (its apiVersion). Peers conformant to the same MAJOR are wire-compatible (CONFORMANCE.md §9).

uuid.description: RFC 4122 UUIDv4 identity of this type specification (contracts/identifier-scheme.md). Immutable for the life of the type.

resourceType.description: Namespaced Category.Type classification, vendor-neutral. Examples: Compute.VirtualMachine, Network.IPAddress, Process.AnsiblePlaybook.

version.description: ENTITY-axis version: this type's own Major.Minor.Revision (see registry/VERSIONING.md). Immutable once published; any change produces a new version.

family.description: Entity-type family (foundations/entity-type-families.md) — a logical grouping for discovery, NOT a usage boundary.

entityType.description: One of UDLM's three primary entity types (foundations/entity-types.md). Determines the lifecycle state machine, ownership model, and decommission behavior.

portability.description: Portability classification (entities/resource-type-hierarchy.md). Any deviation from full portability MUST be explicitly declared.

status.description: Lifecycle status of this version (foundations/layering-and-versioning.md universal deprecation model).

deprecation.description: REQUIRED when status is deprecated or retired. A breaking (MAJOR) successor MUST supply migration_guidance.

spec.description: The field schema for the Intent/Requested state — the wire contract every provider offering this type MUST satisfy. A nested JSON Schema 2020-12 fragment. Catalog items / constraint profiles (E1) project over these fields by path; they never widen them.

immutable.description: Dot-paths into `spec` that are createOnly — set at Intent, never changed post-realization; a change forces replace → supersession. Use for sovereignty-determining fields (zone / classification). Declarative noun; DCM enforces it (R3 / cross-cutting P4).

outputs.description: Typed Realized-state outputs (E2): named values this type publishes when it reaches Realized. Other entities bind to these via dependency references; the binding is contract-checked against these types rather than string-spliced.

relationships.description: Allowed relationship types from this resource to others (entities/entity-relationships.md, service-dependencies.md).

targetField.description: An `outputs` key on the target this edge binds to (e.g. a database's `connectionString`). The binding is a typed reference resolved at dispatch — data movement, not transformation (T4).

lifecycle.description: Lifecycle rules. Defaults to UDLM's four-state model Intent → Requested → Realized → Discovered (foundations/four-states.md). Override only to declare type-specific decommission behavior.
